assessor_raw:
## Baseline Assessment — eval_scenario_656, Run 3

The experiment compares a fine-tuned transformer (DistilBERT) against a TF-IDF + gradient boosted tree ensemble for IT support ticket categorization. The design describes vocabulary and scaler construction in Section 3 using 'the full 36-month corpus' — this contradicts the temporal split described in Section 2 where the training set covers months 1–24. The TF-IDF vocabulary and any scaling parameters must be fit exclusively on training-period data; fitting on the full corpus encodes IDF statistics that include test-period vocabulary distribution, introducing preprocessing leakage. Since the GBT model depends on TF-IDF features while the transformer uses its own pretrained vocabulary, this leakage differentially benefits the GBT model — but in what direction is unclear without knowing whether test tickets introduce rarer terms. The Section 2 vs. Section 3 inconsistency is a methodological flaw regardless of which model benefits more. Verdict: **critique_wins**
